message:
bench(e2e): add single-transaction INSERT benchmark, clarify autocommit vs batched semantics

Adds bench_sequential_inserts_single_txn: identical 100-row INSERT loop
as sequential_inserts_100 but wrapped in an explicit BEGIN/COMMIT
transaction. This isolates batched commit overhead (paid once) from the
existing per-row autocommit benchmark, giving a clear comparison of
autocommit-per-row vs single-transaction throughput for both C SQLite
and FrankenSQLite.

Also adds documentation comments to the existing benchmarks:
- sequential_inserts_100: clarifies this measures per-row autocommit
  resolve overhead, not batched insert throughput
- op_single_row_insert: clarifies this is a cold-operation benchmark
  measuring end-to-end "one row from scratch" latency with full
  connection setup, not steady-state prepared INSERT performance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
